contradiction_text: Basic RAG matches or outperforms more processed write strategies in several settings, but under BM25 Summarized Episodes scores higher than Basic RAG.
related_claim_texts: Basic RAG can match or outperform more processed memory-writing approaches in several tested conditions, though not in every retrieval setting.
Basic RAG was strong with cosine and hybrid retrieval, yet under BM25 Summarized Episodes scored higher than Basic RAG.
disclosure: Keep visible that Basic RAG is not uniformly superior; its strength depends on retrieval regime, and BM25 is a concrete exception.

contradiction_text: Retrieval method explains more performance variation than write strategy overall, but write strategy still changes accuracy by 3 to 8 points within a fixed retrieval setting, so writing is smaller than retrieval effects rather than irrelevant.
related_claim_texts: In this study, retrieval method explains more of the accuracy variation than write strategy does.
A visible tension remains because some write-strategy differences still appear in specific settings, so the result is dominance of retrieval effects, not irrelevance of writing choices.
disclosure: Keep visible that retrieval is the dominant lever in this benchmark, but write choices still matter at nontrivial margins in some configurations.

contradiction_text: Retrieval failure is the largest observed bottleneck, but its magnitude varies sharply across configurations, so the bottleneck ranking is not fixed as a universal property of memory agents.
related_claim_texts: Retrieval failure is a major bottleneck in these memory agents, while utilization failures and hallucinations are comparatively smaller error sources.
The size of retrieval failure varies substantially across configurations, so the bottleneck is configuration-sensitive rather than fixed.
disclosure: Keep visible that retrieval is the main error source only within the tested model, benchmark, retrieval budget, and evaluation setup.
